commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
That is An interpretation of the Vulgate. Belial is sometimes rendered "devilish, apostate "Septuagint, "lawless, or transgressors. "(Menochius)
Aquila, "rebels. "Symmachus, "libertines "without education or restraint. (Calmet)
Josephus lays the blame on some young men, who had been captivated with the charms of the Levite's wife, whom they had seen in the street. But they seem to have had designs still more criminal, though they were prevailed upon to desist, when she was abandoned to them. (Haydock)
The demanded the Levite himself. (Calmet)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
